issues.description: The git diff argument order was swapped from `from_sha, to_sha` to `to_sha, from_sha`. This reverses the diff direction: files Added on main since old_base will be classified as Deleted (and filtered out by --diff-filter=ACMR), while files Deleted on main will be classified as Added (and incorrectly included). The existing caller in workflow.py:860 passes `(old_base, current_main)` expecting 'what changed on main since our base' — the reversed order returns the wrong file set, breaking inter-iteration rebase change detection.
issues.suggested_fix: Change the argument list back to `['git', 'diff', '--name-only', diff_filter, from_sha, to_sha]` to preserve the original (correct) semantics.

issues.description: get_diff_stats silently returns {insertions: 0, deletions: 0} if the git command fails (non-zero exit code) or produces unexpected output. The return code from _run is discarded. If git diff fails (e.g., invalid sha), the caller gets zeros instead of an error, which could mask problems.
issues.suggested_fix: Check the return code from _run and either raise or log a warning on failure, similar to how other methods in this class handle _run errors.

issues.description: int(part.split()[0]) will raise IndexError if a comma-separated part is empty (e.g., trailing comma in git output), and ValueError if the first token is not an integer. While unlikely with standard git output, this is unguarded.
issues.suggested_fix: Wrap the parsing in a try/except (ValueError, IndexError) block, or validate that part.split() is non-empty and the first element is numeric before calling int().

opus_bug_hunter.summary: The diff contains a blocking bug: the sha argument order in get_changed_files was swapped from `[from_sha, to_sha]` to `[to_sha, from_sha]`, reversing the git diff direction. Combined with the `--diff-filter=ACMR` flag, this means files added on main are now classified as deletions and filtered out, while deleted files are classified as additions and included — returning the wrong set of changed files. The existing caller in workflow.py (inter-iteration rebase detection) will get incorrect results. The new get_diff_stats method is functionally correct but lacks error handling for git command failures and has minor parsing fragility.

issues.description: The git diff argument order was swapped from `from_sha, to_sha` to `to_sha, from_sha`, reversing the diff direction. Combined with the new `--diff-filter=ACMR`, this inverts the filter semantics: files Added in from→to appear as 'D' in the reversed direction and are excluded, while files Deleted in from→to appear as 'A' and are included. The function's behavior is the opposite of its documented intent. The existing caller in workflow.py:860 (`get_changed_files(old_base, current_main)`) will now miss files added on main and incorrectly include deleted files.
issues.suggested_fix: Restore the original argument order: `['git', 'diff', '--name-only', diff_filter, from_sha, to_sha]`

issues.description: Previously `get_changed_files` returned ALL changed files (no `--diff-filter`). Now it defaults to `--diff-filter=ACMR`, silently excluding deleted files for existing callers. The caller in workflow.py:860 checks what files changed between old_base and current_main for rebase awareness — it may need to know about deletions too. This is a backward-incompatible default change.
issues.suggested_fix: Consider defaulting `include_deleted=True` to preserve backward compatibility, or audit all existing callers to confirm they don't need deleted files.

issues.description: `get_diff_stats` duplicates the `--shortstat` parsing logic already present in `evals/metrics.py:138` (`_git_diff_stats`). Both extract insertions/deletions from git diff stat output using slightly different approaches (string splitting vs regex). The evals version additionally extracts files_changed. This is a missed reuse opportunity.
issues.suggested_fix: Extract a shared stat-parsing utility (e.g., in git_ops or a shared module) that both `get_diff_stats` and `evals/metrics._git_diff_stats` can call, or have `_git_diff_stats` delegate to `GitOps.get_diff_stats`.

opus_design_critic.summary: The diff has one blocking bug and two design suggestions. The blocking issue is that the `git diff` argument order was swapped from `from_sha, to_sha` to `to_sha, from_sha`, which reverses the diff direction. When combined with the newly added `--diff-filter=ACMR`, this inverts the filter semantics — added files are excluded and deleted files are included, the exact opposite of the documented intent. The existing caller in `workflow.py:860` will silently receive wrong results. Additionally, adding `--diff-filter` as a non-optional behavior changes the contract for existing callers who previously received all changed files. Finally, the new `get_diff_stats` method duplicates stat-parsing logic already present in `evals/metrics.py`.
